body:
# magic.cr

Crystal bindings for `libmagic`, the library used by the Unix `file` command
to identify file types from their contents.

The shard provides a small Crystal-friendly wrapper for common use and exposes
the raw C API through `LibMagic` when you need direct access.

## Requirements

- Crystal `>= 1.16.0`
- `libmagic`
- The libmagic development headers

Install libmagic before building applications that depend on this shard:

```sh
# Debian/Ubuntu
sudo apt install libmagic-dev

# Fedora
sudo dnf install file-devel

# Arch
sudo pacman -S file

# macOS
brew install libmagic
```

## Installation

Add the shard to your `shard.yml`:

```yaml
dependencies:
  magic:
    github: trans/magic.cr
    version: ~> 0.1.1
```

Then install dependencies:

```sh
shards install
```

## Usage

```crystal
require "magic"

puts Magic.file("README.md")
# => ASCII text

puts Magic.file("README.md", Magic::Flag::MimeType)
# => text/plain

puts Magic.buffer("hello\n")
# => ASCII text
```

For repeated lookups, keep a database handle open:

```crystal
Magic.open do |magic|
  puts magic.file("README.md")
  puts magic.buffer("hello\n")
end
```

You can pass libmagic flags when opening a handle:

```crystal
Magic.open(Magic::Flag::MimeType | Magic::Flag::Symlink) do |magic|
  puts magic.file("README.md")
end
```

Or use a one-shot helper:

```crystal
mime_type = Magic.file("README.md", Magic::Flag::MimeType)
```

## API

### One-shot helpers

```crystal
Magic.file(path, flags = Magic::Flag::None, database = nil)
Magic.buffer(bytes, flags = Magic::Flag::None, database = nil)
Magic.buffer(string, flags = Magic::Flag::None, database = nil)
Magic.version
```

These helpers open libmagic, load the database, run one lookup, and close the
handle.

### Reusable database handle

```crystal
magic = Magic::Database.open(Magic::Flag::MimeType)
magic.file("README.md")
magic.buffer("hello\n")
magic.descriptor(fd)
magic.close
```

Prefer the block form so the handle is always closed:

```crystal
Magic::Database.open(Magic::Flag::MimeType) do |magic|
  puts magic.file("README.md")
end
```

A custom magic database path can be passed as the second argument:

```crystal
Magic.open(Magic::Flag::None, "/path/to/magic.mgc") do |magic|
  puts magic.file("sample.bin")
end
```

### Flags

`Magic::Flag` maps the libmagic flags into a Crystal flags enum. Common values
include:

- `Magic::Flag::MimeType`
- `Magic::Flag::MimeEncoding`
- `Magic::Flag::Mime`
- `Magic::Flag::Symlink`
- `Magic::Flag::Compress`
- `Magic::Flag::Error`
- `Magic::Flag::Extension`

Flags can be combined with `|`:

```crystal
flags = Magic::Flag::MimeType | Magic::Flag::Error
puts Magic.file("README.md", flags)
```

## Errors

libmagic failures raise `Magic::Error`:

```crystal
begin
  Magic.file("missing.file", Magic::Flag::Error)
rescue error : Magic::Error
  puts error.message
  puts error.errno
end
```

## Raw LibMagic API

The low-level binding is available as `LibMagic`:

```crystal
handle = LibMagic.magic_open(LibMagic::MAGIC_MIME_TYPE)
LibMagic.magic_load(handle, Pointer(LibC::Char).null)
puts String.new(LibMagic.magic_file(handle, "README.md"))
LibMagic.magic_close(handle)
```

Use this layer when you need a libmagic function that the wrapper does not
abstract.

## Development

Run the specs:

```sh
CRYSTAL_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/crystal-cache crystal spec
```

Format source and specs:

```sh
crystal tool format src spec
```

## License

MIT. See [LICENSE](LICENSE).

## Contributors

- [Thomas Sawyer](https://github.com/trans) - creator and maintainer

types.name: Magic
types.doc:
High-level Crystal wrapper for libmagic.

Use this module for normal Crystal code. It opens libmagic handles, loads the
magic database, converts C strings into Crystal strings, raises `Magic::Error`
on failure, and closes handles automatically when using the block APIs.

Use `LibMagic` directly when you need exact access to the underlying C API.

class_methods.name: buffer
class_methods.doc:
One-shot byte-slice identification helper.

Opens a database handle, loads the database, identifies *bytes*, closes the
handle, and returns the libmagic result string.
class_methods.args_string: (bytes : Bytes, flags : Flag | Int32 = Flag::None, database : String | Path | Nil = nil) : String
class_methods.args_html: (bytes : Bytes, flags : Flag | Int32 = Flag::None, database : String | Path | Nil = nil) : String

class_methods.name: buffer
class_methods.args_string: (string : String, flags : Flag | Int32 = Flag::None, database : String | Path | Nil = nil) : String
class_methods.args_html: (string : String, flags : Flag | Int32 = Flag::None, database : String | Path | Nil = nil) : String

class_methods.name: file
class_methods.doc:
One-shot file identification helper.

Opens a database handle, loads the database, identifies *path*, closes the
handle, and returns the libmagic result string.
class_methods.args_string: (path : String | Path, flags : Flag | Int32 = Flag::None, database : String | Path | Nil = nil) : String
class_methods.args_html: (path : String | Path, flags : Flag | Int32 = Flag::None, database : String | Path | Nil = nil) : String

class_methods.name: open
class_methods.args_string: (flags : Flag | Int32 = Flag::None, database : String | Path | Nil = nil, load : Bool = true) : Database
class_methods.args_html: (flags : Flag | Int32 = Flag::None, database : String | Path | Nil = nil, load : Bool = true) : Database

class_methods.name: open
class_methods.doc: Opens a reusable database handle, yields it, and closes it after the block.
class_methods.summary: Opens a reusable database handle, yields it, and closes it after the block.
class_methods.args_string: (flags : Flag | Int32 = Flag::None, database : String | Path | Nil = nil, load : Bool = true, &)
class_methods.args_html: (flags : Flag | Int32 = Flag::None, database : String | Path | Nil = nil, load : Bool = true, &)

def.name: open
def.body:
Database.open(flags, database, load) do |database_handle|
  yield database_handle
end

class_methods.name: version
class_methods.doc: Returns the linked libmagic version number.

types.name: Database
types.doc:
Reusable libmagic database handle.

A database owns a `LibMagic::Handle`. Use `Magic.open` or
`Magic::Database.open` with a block when possible so the handle is closed
deterministically.

class_methods.name: open
class_methods.doc: Opens a database handle, yields it, and closes it after the block exits.
class_methods.summary: Opens a database handle, yields it, and closes it after the block exits.
class_methods.args_string: (flags : Flag | Int32 = Flag::None, database : String | Path | Nil = nil, load : Bool = true, &)
class_methods.args_html: (flags : Flag | Int32 = Flag::None, database : String | Path | Nil = nil, load : Bool = true, &)

def.name: open
def.body:
database_handle = new(flags, database, load)
begin
  yield database_handle
ensure
  database_handle.close
end

constructors.name: new
constructors.doc:
Creates a database handle.

Pass *load* as `false` when you need to call `#load`, `#check`,
`#compile`, or `#list` manually before running lookups.
constructors.args_string: (flags : Flag | Int32 = Flag::None, database : String | Path | Nil = nil, load : Bool = true)
constructors.args_html: (flags : Flag | Int32 = Flag::None, database : String | Path | Nil = nil, load : Bool = true)

constructors.name: open
constructors.doc:
Opens a reusable database handle.

If *load* is `true`, the default magic database or the given *database*
path is loaded immediately.
constructors.args_string: (flags : Flag | Int32 = Flag::None, database : String | Path | Nil = nil, load : Bool = true) : self
constructors.args_html: (flags : Flag | Int32 = Flag::None, database : String | Path | Nil = nil, load : Bool = true) : self

instance_methods.name: buffer
instance_methods.doc: Identifies a byte slice and returns the libmagic result string.
instance_methods.summary: Identifies a byte slice and returns the libmagic result string.

instance_methods.name: buffer
instance_methods.doc: Identifies a string's bytes and returns the libmagic result string.
instance_methods.summary: Identifies a string's bytes and returns the libmagic result string.

instance_methods.name: check
instance_methods.doc: Checks the default magic database or the database at *database*.
instance_methods.summary: Checks the default magic database or the database at database.

def.name: check
def.body:
if database
  database_path = database.to_s
  check(LibMagic.magic_check(handle, database_path))
else
  check(LibMagic.magic_check(handle, Pointer(LibC::Char).null))
end

self

instance_methods.name: close
instance_methods.doc:
Closes the underlying libmagic handle.

Calling this more than once is safe.

def.name: close
def.body:
if closed?
  return
end

LibMagic.magic_close(@handle)
@handle = Pointer(Void).null

instance_methods.name: closed?
instance_methods.doc: Returns `true` if the underlying libmagic handle has been closed.
instance_methods.summary: Returns true if the underlying libmagic handle has been closed.

instance_methods.name: compile
instance_methods.doc: Compiles the default magic database or the database at *database*.
instance_methods.summary: Compiles the default magic database or the database at database.

def.name: compile
def.body:
if database
  database_path = database.to_s
  check(LibMagic.magic_compile(handle, database_path))
else
  check(LibMagic.magic_compile(handle, Pointer(LibC::Char).null))
end

self

instance_methods.name: descriptor
instance_methods.doc: Identifies the data available from an open file descriptor.
instance_methods.summary: Identifies the data available from an open file descriptor.

instance_methods.name: file
instance_methods.doc: Identifies a file by path and returns the libmagic result string.
instance_methods.summary: Identifies a file by path and returns the libmagic result string.

instance_methods.name: finalize
instance_methods.doc: Closes the handle during garbage collection if it was not closed already.
instance_methods.summary: Closes the handle during garbage collection if it was not closed already.

instance_methods.name: flags
instance_methods.doc: Returns the current libmagic flags as an integer bitmask.
instance_methods.summary: Returns the current libmagic flags as an integer bitmask.

instance_methods.name: flags=
instance_methods.doc: Replaces the current libmagic flags.

instance_methods.name: list
instance_methods.doc:
Prints a human-readable listing of the default magic database or the
database at *database*.
instance_methods.summary: Prints a human-readable listing of the default magic database or the database at database.

def.name: list
def.body:
if database
  database_path = database.to_s
  check(LibMagic.magic_list(handle, database_path))
else
  check(LibMagic.magic_list(handle, Pointer(LibC::Char).null))
end

self

instance_methods.name: load
instance_methods.doc: Loads the default magic database or the database at *database*.
instance_methods.summary: Loads the default magic database or the database at database.

def.name: load
def.body:
if database
  database_path = database.to_s
  check(LibMagic.magic_load(handle, database_path))
else
  check(LibMagic.magic_load(handle, Pointer(LibC::Char).null))
end

self

types.name: Error
types.doc:
Exception raised for libmagic failures.

When libmagic reports an operating system error, `#errno` contains that
errno value. Otherwise it is `nil`.

instance_methods.name: errno
instance_methods.doc: Operating system errno reported by libmagic, when available.
instance_methods.summary: Operating system errno reported by libmagic, when available.

types.name: Flag

constants.name: Compress
constants.doc: Inspect compressed files by decompressing their contents.
constants.summary: Inspect compressed files by decompressing their contents.

constants.name: MimeType
constants.doc: Return a MIME type, such as `text/plain`.

constants.name: Continue
constants.doc: Return all matches instead of only the first match.
constants.summary: Return all matches instead of only the first match.

constants.name: Check
constants.doc: Check magic database consistency and print warnings to stderr.
constants.summary: Check magic database consistency and print warnings to stderr.

constants.name: PreserveAtime
constants.doc: Try to preserve file access times where supported.
constants.summary: Try to preserve file access times where supported.

constants.name: Error
constants.doc: Treat filesystem errors as lookup errors.

constants.name: MimeEncoding
constants.doc: Return the MIME encoding, such as `us-ascii`.

constants.name: Mime
constants.doc: Return MIME type and MIME encoding.

constants.name: Apple
constants.doc: Return Apple creator and type information.

constants.name: CompressTransp
constants.doc: Inspect compressed files without reporting the compression wrapper.
constants.summary: Inspect compressed files without reporting the compression wrapper.

constants.name: NoCheckSoft
constants.doc: Do not consult magic database entries.

constants.name: NoCheckCdf
constants.doc: Do not inspect CDF / Microsoft compound document data.
constants.summary: Do not inspect CDF / Microsoft compound document data.

constants.name: NoCheckTokens
constants.doc: Do not inspect known tokens in text data.
constants.summary: Do not inspect known tokens in text data.

constants.name: NoCheckSimh
constants.doc: Do not inspect SIMH tape data.

constants.name: NoCheckBuiltin
constants.doc: Disable built-in checks while still allowing database checks.
constants.summary: Disable built-in checks while still allowing database checks.

types.doc:
Flags that control libmagic lookup behavior.

The enum maps directly to the `MAGIC_*` constants from libmagic. Values can
be combined with `|`.

```
flags = Magic::Flag::MimeType | Magic::Flag::Error
puts Magic.file("README.md", flags)
```
